frameworks.name: optimal control
frameworks.one_line: learning rules as policies for navigating partially observable loss landscapes

frameworks.name: online Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: adaptive optimizers cast as inference of loss-landscape shape

frameworks.name: gradient descent
frameworks.one_line: emerges as the short-horizon special case of the control policy

frameworks.name: natural gradient methods
frameworks.one_line: arise from accounting for parameter-space geometry

core_question: Under what assumptions can a given parameter-update rule be considered optimal for navigating a loss landscape?
key_assumptions: learning is policy-based navigation over a (partially observable) loss landscape
optimality is defined by an associated optimal control objective
loss-landscape shape can be estimated online from observations
different horizons and observability regimes select different optimal policies
key_findings: gradient descent recovered from short-horizon optimization
natural gradients recovered from parameter-space geometry
Adam-like adaptive rules recovered from online Bayesian inference
interpretations: diverse learning rules share a single normative objective
continual-learning tactics like weight resetting are optimal responses to task uncertainty
